mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market and expanding economy, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:strong employment growth in recent months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which in turn supports corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.3% is lower than recent peaks
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the average 60d return during triggered periods is not statistically significant from zero (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is exceptionally low while retail sales show strong growth, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment supporting robust consumer demand, which is highly favorable for equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and strong RSAFS growth
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with loose monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and RSAFS growth is not statistically significant in predicting returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust economic expansion, accelerating industrial production signals strong manufacturing activity and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate continued growth.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and SPX returns turns negative over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts indicates a strengthening housing market and broader economic confidence, which stimulates related industries and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the average 20d return during triggered periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by the Consumer Price Index, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy and thus negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL shows positive year-over-year growth above 2.5%, or Rank IC > 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling unemployment and strong payroll growth signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and robust consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls month-over-month AND PAYEMS growth > 100k
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of UNRATE and PAYEMS is not statistically significant in predicting returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction or significant slowdown, a weak or declining GDP growth rate signals deteriorating corporate fundamentals and investor sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are revised downwards.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the average 60d return during triggered periods is not statistically significant from zero (p > 0.1).
